states.name: EscapeMenu
states.notes: Escape menu with options, career stats, and exit buttons. Not expected to be used by command execution.

states.name: GameplayNoMenu
states.notes: Game is running and no detected UI menu is open.

states.name: BlankMenu
states.notes: Blank command-style menu with a white line. Mostly observed in headquarters, but may occur elsewhere.

states.name: InteractionPrompt
states.notes: F interaction prompt. Currently appears to take priority over command menu state when looking at interactable objects.

states.name: IndividualSwatCommandMenu
states.notes: Appears when looking at an individual SWAT team member while opening the command menu.

commands.name: Focus
commands.variantNotes: Conditional. Sometimes unavailable if the selected SWAT AI is executing a command that requires movement or mobility.

commands.name: SwapWith
commands.variantNotes: Conditional. Available when the SWAT team is in active formation, stacked on a door, or actively holding a position.

commands.name: Door
commands.variantNotes: Only available when actively looking at a door or doorway.

commands.name: Target
commands.variantNotes: Only available when actively looking at an enemy or civilian NPC.

commands.name: Unfocus
commands.variantNotes: Conditional and unavailable by default. Cancels previous focus commands.

states.name: CivilianSuspectCommandMenu
states.notes: Appears when looking at a dead, incapacitated, or surrendered NPC, excluding SWAT AI.

commands.name: Deploy
commands.variantNotes: Observed grayed out by default most of the time. Submenu value not identified yet.

commands.name: Stop
commands.variantNotes: Only available if the NPC was previously ordered to move.

states.name: DoorwayCommandMenu
states.notes: Appears on doorways or room transitions with no physical door.

commands.name: Breach
commands.variantNotes: Currently observed as unavailable. Need to verify whether it can become enabled.

states.name: ThroughOpeningCommandMenu
states.notes: Appears when looking through an open door or doorway at another door/doorway. Rarely used; basic mapping only.

commands.name: Doorway
commands.variantNotes: When looking through to a door, text becomes Door and opens the normal DoorCommandMenu.

commands.name: OtherDoorway
commands.variantNotes: When looking through to a door, text becomes OtherDoor and opens the normal DoorCommandMenu.

variants.name: DoorTarget
variants.notes: Doorway/OtherDoorway labels are replaced by Door/OtherDoor, and the next state is the normal DoorCommandMenu.

states.name: GroundCommandMenu
states.notes: Appears when the player looks at the ground or a wall that does not trigger another command menu type.

commands.name: Open
commands.variantNotes: If the door is locked, this slot is replaced by PickLock and does not open a submenu.

commands.name: WedgeDoor
commands.variantNotes: If a trap is present, DisarmTrap is inserted at key 6 and this shifts to key 7. If the door is already wedged/jammed, this slot is replaced by RemoveWedge/RemoveDoorJam.

commands.name: Cover
commands.variantNotes: If a trap is present, this shifts to key 8.

commands.name: OpenDoor
commands.variantNotes: If a trap is present, this shifts to key 9. If the targeted door is already open, this slot is replaced by CloseDoor.

variants.name: LockedDoor
variants.notes: Open is replaced by PickLock at key 2 and does not open DoorOpenSubmenu.

variants.name: TrappedDoor
variants.notes: A trap command is inserted at key 6, shifting WedgeDoor, Cover, and OpenDoor one slot later.

variants.name: OpenDoor
variants.notes: When the targeted door is already open, key 8 is CloseDoor instead of OpenDoor. If trap-related slot shifting can coexist with this, the final key should be verified separately.

variants.name: JammedDoor
variants.notes: When the targeted door is already wedged/jammed, the WedgeDoor command is replaced by RemoveWedge/RemoveDoorJam in the same slot.

states.name: GroundFallInSubmenu
states.notes: Shares the same memory value as DoorStackUpSubmenu but has different visible text because it is reached from GroundCommandMenu.

states.name: DoorBreachSubmenu
states.notes: Every breach option opens the already mapped DoorOpenSubmenu.

states.name: ThroughOpeningDoorwaySubmenu
states.notes: Shares the same memory value as DoorBreachSubmenu, but in this parent context it represents a stripped-down doorway/door command menu.

commands.name: Breach
commands.variantNotes: Currently treated as uncertain/unavailable until tested.
